title: The Whispered Ledger in Whitby Hall
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned antiquarian, was found dead in the modest library with a lamp, a book, a mug and a chair. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Victor Harcourt
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the lamp and did not go near the book.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Clara Finch
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Dr. Malcolm Reeves helped me move the chair clear of the doorway. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Victor Harcourt and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Dr. Malcolm Reeves and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Dr. Malcolm Reeves
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Clara Finch move the chair clear of the doorway. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Clara Finch and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

clues.title: The Dented Lamp
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the lamp. Eleanor Whitcombe has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Movement of Chair
clues.detail: A fresh floor scrape made by the chair is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Clara Finch and Dr. Malcolm Reeves both state that they moved the chair clear of the doorway at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Lamp
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught inside a fresh split beneath the base of the lamp. Victor Harcourt's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Book
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged between the cover and first page of the book on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Victor Harcourt has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Mug
clues.detail: A crack runs across the mug. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Victor Harcourt killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the imminent exposure of his embezzlement of the society's funds.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitcombe was killed by a heavy blow from the lamp.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Victor Harcourt claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Clara Finch and Dr. Malcolm Reeves left together at 8:35 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the lamp and the black and gold fragment recovered from the book place Victor Harcourt inside afterwards. Victor Harcourt therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitcombe and reach the lamp during the murder window.
